lede: A look at September 5 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Bukayo Saka and Pablo Granoche, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Liz Truss
events.desc: Liz Truss is declared the winner of the UK Conservative Party leadership election, beating Rishi Sunak.

events.title: Gotthard Road Tunnel
events.desc: The Gotthard Road Tunnel opens in Switzerland as the world's longest highway tunnel at 16.9 kilometres (10.5 mi) stretching from Göschenen to Airolo.

events.title: Cold War
events.desc: Cold War: Igor Gouzenko, a Soviet Union embassy clerk, defects to Canada, exposing Soviet espionage in North America, signalling the beginning of the Cold War.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: Japanese high command orders withdrawal at Milne Bay, the first major Japanese defeat in land warfare during the Pacific War.

events.title: Exeter Theatre Royal fire
events.desc: A fire at the Theatre Royal, Exeter kills 186, making it the UK's deadliest ever building fire.

events.title: First Opium War
events.desc: The United Kingdom declares war on the Qing dynasty of China.

events.title: Louis XVIII
events.desc: Louis XVIII of France has to dissolve the Chambre introuvable ("Unobtainable Chamber").

events.title: Swa Saw Ke
events.desc: Swa Saw Ke becomes king of Ava

events.title: Liu Yan (emperor)
events.desc: Liu Yan declares himself emperor, establishing the Southern Han state in southern China, at his capital of Panyu.

births.name: Willie Gault
births.line: American football player, athlete, and actor

births.name: Gareth Evans (politician)
births.line: Australian lawyer and politician, 33rd Australian Minister of Foreign Affairs

observances.text: Christian feast day: Zechariah and Elisabeth (Anglican and Eastern Orthodox Church)
